bead.title: Wire required execution gates into server execute-loop coordinator landing path
bead.description: Server execute-loop path in cli/internal/server/workers.go (around line 388-395) builds a LandRequest via BuildLandRequestFromResult and submits it directly through coordinator.Submit, bypassing LandBeadResult and gate evaluation entirely. Land() is intentionally a pure ref-advance contract and should not learn about gates. Choose one of two designs: (a) extend the coordinator pattern so Submit orchestrates gate-eval -> land-or-preserve, or (b) run gate evaluation upstream in workers.go before submission and convert the request to preserve-only when a required gate fails. Manifest path is available from the ExecuteBead result; reuse ExtractGoverningIDsFromManifest.
bead.acceptance:
- [ ] Server-managed execute-loop runs evaluateRequiredGates against the result worktree before the coordinator advances the target ref
- [ ] A required failing gate preserves the attempt under refs/ddx/iterations/, writes gate results into the execution bundle, and surfaces post_run_check_failed via the loop event
- [ ] A required passing gate still merges through the coordinator and records the pass summary
- [ ] Regression test in cli/internal/server/ covers required-pass and required-fail fixtures driven through the server worker path
- [ ] No-gate case still merges (backward compatible)
